title: 10 best herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for over bleeding during periods are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate excessive menstrual bleeding. These oils contain bioactive compounds that regulate hormonal balance and promote uterine health, thereby reducing bleeding. The best herbal essential oils for over bleeding include Vitex agnus-castus, Cimicifuga racemosa, Paeonia lactiflora, Ginkgo biloba, Angelica archangelica, Trifolium pratense, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Angelica sinensis, Paeonia officinalis, and Melissa officinalis. If left untreated, over bleeding can lead to anemia, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with over bleeding during periods because it has natural antispasmodic properties that can help to ease cramps and reduce heavy menstrual flow. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also work to constrict blood vessels, thus reducing excessive bleeding. Additionally, sage oil's astringent properties help to tone the uterine lining, which can become weak or thin due to hormonal fluctuations during menstruation, leading to heavier bleeding. By using sage essential oils, women may experience relief from prolonged and heavy menstrual periods.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Herbal sage essential oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which may contribute to heavy bleeding, while also protecting against oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive blood loss.

Uterine Tonic and Constrictor Properties: Sage essential oil has been traditionally used as a uterine tonic and constrictor, helping to strengthen uterine muscle contractions and reduce uterine bleeding by promoting healthy uterine function and reducing inflammation.

Astringent and Hemostatic Properties: Herbal sage essential oil's astringent and hemostatic properties can help stop bleeding by constricting blood vessels and drying up excessive discharge, providing relief from heavy menstrual flow and associated discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of sage contain a high concentration of volatile compounds that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
Rhyzomes: Sage's rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients and energy, contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to stop heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia sclarea leaves from your garden or local botanical store weighing approximately 500 grams.
Dry the harvested salvia sclarea leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7 days or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried salvia sclarea leaves with distilled water at a ratio of 1:4 for 4 hours to obtain the essential oil.
Filter the resulting oil mixture through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids to preserve its aroma and properties.
Allow the essential oil to mature for at least 2 weeks before using it topically or aromatically diluted with a carrier oil in concentrations of 2-5%.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the uterus. The oil's gentle, calming effects can reduce cramping and ease discomfort, allowing the uterine lining to shed more smoothly. Additionally, chamomile's astringent properties can help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding and promoting clotting. This natural remedy can provide relief from heavy menstrual flow, making it an effective solution for women experiencing irregular or prolonged bleeding during their periods.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chamomile essential oil helps to relax the uterine muscles, reducing heavy menstrual bleeding by calming cramps and spasms. This reduces the flow of blood and alleviates discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during periods. By reducing inflammation, chamomile essential oil helps to decrease bleeding.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding. By reducing oxidative stress, chamomile essential oil promotes overall uterine health and reduces bleeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are rich in apigenin, an flavonoid responsible for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing menstrual bleeding.
Roots: Chamomile roots contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess hemostatic properties, helping to stop excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers ensuring minimal contamination.
Use a clean and sterilized grinder to crush 200 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder.
Transfer the powder to a glass container with a lid and add 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Place the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing the herbs to steep and infuse the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and store the resulting essential oil in an amber glass bottle with a tight lid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for maximum potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers using shade drying or machine drying methods within 24 hours of harvesting.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 milliliters of water for 4-6 hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil using a coiled glass condenser and coffee filters within an hour of distillation.
Store the resulting essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves in the early morning when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 12 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Steam distill the crushed leaves using 1 kilogram of leaves with 1 liter of water for 3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper and store in dark glass bottle.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh achillea millefolium leaves and stems early morning to ensure highest potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, dry, dark place for 7-10 days or until crumbly to reduce moisture content.
Use a steam distillation apparatus with a ratio of 1:5 (plant material to water) to extract essential oil from dried achillea millefolium for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment within 30 minutes.
Store the extracted achillea millefolium essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn in late summer or early fall when fragrant and rich in oil content.
Cleanly wash the harvested leaves with cold water and gently pat dry with paper towels to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the clean leaves using a copper still and high-quality steam generator for 1-2 hours at atmospheric pressure.
Separate the resulting essential oil from the distilled water by decanting it into a separate container and storing it in an amber glass bottle at room temperature.
Blend the melissa officinalis essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a ratio of 10% to 90% and store it in a dark glass bottle for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers from organic gardens in the morning when petals are fully open ensuring 2 cups of fresh material.

Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction time.

Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing uterine bleeding and alleviating symptoms of heavy menstrual flow.

Terpenes (specifically, Ursolic Acid): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help in relaxing uterine muscles and reducing menstrual cramps associated with heavy bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate menstrual cramps and excessive bleeding.
Leaves: Used because of their flavonoid and polyphenol content, which may help reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms associated with PMS.
Barks: Used due to their triterpenoid and saponin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that may help reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in the early morning to ensure optimal quality.
Dry the roots in a warm place for 2 weeks at a temperature of 35°c to 45°c.
Steam distill the dried roots using a still with a 10 kg capacity for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate using a separator for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter for 10 minutes to ensure purity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis essential oils helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce excessive bleeding by relaxing the uterine muscles and improving circulation. The oil's ability to counteract hormonal imbalances and promote blood clotting also contributes to its effectiveness in addressing heavy menstrual flow. As a result, many women have found relief from excessive bleeding and discomfort associated with heavy periods.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: It helps to reduce bleeding by acting as an anti-inflammatory and vasodilator, improving blood flow and reducing menstrual cramps.
Butylphthalide: It exhibits anti-oxidative and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate excessive bleeding.
Z -ligustilide: It has a similar effect to ligustilide, acting as an anti-inflammatory and vasodilator to improve blood flow and reduce menstrual cramps, thereby reducing excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ferulic acid, which helps to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms associated with menstrual disorders.
Leaves: Contain compounds that help to regulate blood flow and reduce bleeding, making them a popular choice for essential oils.
Stems: Rich in flavonoids and coumarins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate menstrual cramps and bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature angelica sinensis roots in late summer or early fall after the plant has stopped flowering.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris from the harvesting process immediately.
Steam distillation of the roots at 100-110 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours is required to extract essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil and separate it from the water by decantation or centrifugation quickly.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any remaining impurities completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia officinalis essential oils helps with over bleeding during periods because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The oil is rich in tannins, which help to contract blood vessels and reduce blood flow, thereby alleviating excessive menstrual bleeding. Additionally, Paeonia officinalis has a calming effect on the uterus, helping to regulate menstrual cycles and ease cramps. Its natural properties make it an effective remedy for women experiencing heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding, providing relief and promoting a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with over bleeding during periods by exerting their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce blood loss and alleviate discomfort associated with heavy menstrual flow.

Phenolic acids: They help with over bleeding during periods by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions and contribute to excessive bleeding.

Tannins: They help with over bleeding during periods by contracting the uterine muscles, reducing blood loss, and promoting clotting, which helps to alleviate heavy menstrual flow and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds like paeoniflorin and paeonol, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: They contain paeonol, which helps to reduce inflammation and relax the uterine muscles, thereby alleviating menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
Flowers: They contain paeoniflorin, which has a sedative effect and helps to reduce menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and anxiety.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of paeonia officinalis, when mature and dried, at a ratio of 80kg of roots to 30l of water.
Steam distillation is carried out for 2-3 hours at 100-110c, with a steam flow rate of 20l per minute.
The distillate is then collected and separated into essential oil and hydrosol, with the essential oil making up 0.5-1.5% of total distillate.
The essential oil is then filtered through activated charcoal to remove impurities and improve color.
The filtered oil is then stored in dark glass containers to preserve quality and prevent degradation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils helps with over bleeding during periods because of its unique properties. The oil's high content of linalool and linalyl acetate helps to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce excessive bleeding. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties also help to ease cramps and calm the uterus, reducing the severity of bleeding. Additionally, the oil's ability to improve circulation and stimulate the uterus's natural contractions helps to reduce bleeding and promote a healthy menstrual flow. This natural remedy provides relief from heavy periods.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a mild anxiolytic and relaxant, helping to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to excessive menstrual bleeding.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus, potentially alleviating heavy menstrual bleeding.
Neryl acetate: Has a mild uterine relaxant effect, which can help to reduce uterine contractions and alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are used due to their high content of estrogen-like compounds that help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding.
Flowers: Melissa officinalis flowers are used due to their ability to balance hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to excessive menstrual bleeding.
Buds: Melissa officinalis buds are used due to their potential to stimulate uterine contractions and help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently brushing off dirt and debris from their surface.
Steam distill the cleaned melissa officinalis leaves to extract their essential oil.
Collect the extracted essential oil from the distillation apparatus and store it in a dark glass bottle.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities.
